Description: JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint for tool discovery and invocation - errors are returned as a JSON-RPC error envelope inside an HTTP 200 response not as 4xx status codes
AuthScopes: none enforced - bearer JWT via Depends(get_current_user)
Notes: tools/call handler has a hard 60s asyncio timeout - tool_rag.py semantic selection is a separate discovery path not part of tools/call itself

Description: Server-sent events bus for tool and workflow updates
Registers a new workflow definition for topological execution - matches the real route in workers/workflow-engine-service/worker.py
Notes: No version prefix in the implemented route - internal-auth only via require_internal_auth

Summary: Exchange an authorization code for an access token
Description: OAuth2 token endpoint supporting the authorization_code grant with PKCE - matches workers/infinity-auth/router.py
Notes: client_credentials is not implemented - the refresh_token grant is also broken in the current code since it queries a refresh_tokens table that is never created (only users/sessions/rate_limits/auth_codes exist) - document authorization_code only until that is fixed

Description: Returns secret metadata (key name tags ttl version) with access logging - does NOT return the decrypted value - matches workers/vault-service/worker.py SecretResponse model
Notes: No /api/v1 prefix in the implemented route - internal-auth only - no separate endpoint currently exposes the decrypted secret value

Description: Executes a LogQL expression against Loki and returns the latest matching log lines - matches workers/observatory/router.py
Notes: Not a dedicated audit-log API - Observatory has no /audit-logs route - the implementation performs a fresh Loki query on every call with no pagination or response caching - see also /backends /traces /metrics /node /dashboard
